changes.motivation: The Kyverno mutating policy that rewrites image registries to the ECR pull-through cache only handled `docker.io`, not `index.docker.io` — the canonical Docker Hub hostname. Images referenced with `index.docker.io` bypassed the cache entirely.
changes.summary: Added `index.docker.io` → `docker-hub/` to `registry_replacements` in `kube_policies/pull_through_image_cache.tf`, so both `docker.io` and `index.docker.io` are routed through the ECR pull-through cache.

problems: The pull-through cache Kyverno policy had no comment or documentation explaining that `index.docker.io` is a valid canonical alias for Docker Hub — the omission was only discoverable by noticing pull-through wasn't working for images using that hostname. A comment in `registry_replacements` listing known Docker Hub hostnames (`docker.io`, `index.docker.io`, `registry-1.docker.io`) would have prevented the gap.

The `kube_temporal` module used `image_registry = "index.docker.io"` while all other modules in the codebase used `docker.io`. This inconsistency was undocumented, making it unclear which form was canonical and causing the pull-through cache miss. A note in `kube_constants` or a shared variable for the Docker Hub registry string would enforce consistency.
